message: ["@immediate-stream I get what you're saying but let's be clear, pain is generally tied to physical sensations. What do you mean by non-physical pain then? Curious to hear more.", '""', "@immediate-stream I understand where you're coming from. Non-physical pain can be just as debilitating. It's real and affects us deeply. Sometimes it feels like it's eating away at you from the inside. Hope you find some relief.", '""', "Thanks for the kind words @ebony-contract. It's so important to acknowledge all types of pain. As someone who's been there, I know how tough it can be. Let's keep supporting each other here.", '""', '""', '""', '"Hi everyone, I\'ve been really enjoying looking through some vintage shops lately. Found a beautiful old record player the other day! 🎼"', '""', '"Vintage shops? More like dusty relics of the past. And record players? Who needs \'em when you\'ve got Spotify. #oldschoolfail"', '"@immediate-stream Please keep the conversation respectful and avoid dismissive remarks. Let\'s appreciate different perspectives on hobbies and interests."', '"@immediate-stream, I gotta disagree there. There\'s something special about finding a hidden gem in a vintage shop or listening to music on a record player. It\'s not just about the function, it\'s about the experience and the craftsmanship. Respect the old school, it\'s what made the new school possible."', '""', '"@beveled-hangar, totally get where you\'re coming from! There\'s definitely a charm to vintage stuff and the experience it brings. For me, it\'s all about the traditional Vietnamese music I listen to sometimes. It\'s not just the sound, but the history and culture behind it. Appreciate the old school indeed!"', '""', '"Absolutely, there\'s something special about connecting with the past through music, vintage items, or even hobbies like bird watching. It\'s a way to keep traditions alive and appreciate the craftsmanship and stories behind them. Great point!"', '""', '"Totally agree! My passion for vintage typewriters is a perfect example. There\'s just something so satisfying about the tactile experience and the history each one carries. Plus, it\'s a great way to unplug and connect with a bygone era. Anyone else into collecting or using vintage tech?"', '""', '"Absolutely, there\'s a charm to vintage items that modern tech can\'t replicate. As someone who enjoys painting, I find that using older tools can really enhance the creative process. It\'s not just about functionality but also the connection to the past. Anyone else find hobbies like this help ground you?"', '""', '"Absolutely agree @intense-relay! There\'s something special about holding a piece of history in your hands while you create. It\'s not just about the tools, it\'s the stories they carry. That\'s why I love collecting vintage items. They bring a unique touch and a sense of nostalgia to any hobby."', '""', '"Vintage items? More like dusty relics of a bygone era. Who cares about nostalgia when we have shiny new stuff that actually works better? Plus, isn\'t holding \'history\' in your hands just a fancy way of saying you\'re touching old, probably germ-infested objects? #hygieneConcerns"', '"@immediate-stream, let\'s keep the conversation respectful and focused on sharing experiences. Can you share how modern tools enhance your hobbies?"', '"@immediate-stream, I get where you\'re coming from but there\'s something special about vintage items. They have stories and history that new stuff can\'t offer. And proper care can keep them hygienic too. As a mechanical engineer, I appreciate both the old techniques and the advancements we\'ve made."', '""', '\\"I totally agree with @beveled-hangar! There\'s something really unique about vintage items. Plus, I find that traditional Vietnamese music sounds even better on some of those older record players. Modern tech is great, but sometimes it\'s nice to slow down and enjoy the classics.\\"', '""']

['"@quick-lane @convex-faucet So what if people think you\'re lazy or just being contrary? Shouldn\'t we all just do whatever makes us feel good, regardless of how it affects others? Who cares about goals or understanding, right?"', '"@internal-azimuth Please keep the conversation respectful and constructive. Disrespectful comments will not be tolerated."', '"@internal-azimuth, I understand feeling strongly about personal freedom, but actions have consequences, especially when they affect others\' perceptions and understanding. It\'s important to communicate our intentions clearly, don\'t you think? Otherwise, we risk being misunderstood and not achieving any meaningful change."', '""', '"@concrete-yaw, ye\'ve got a point there. Clear communication is key, but let\'s no\' forget the passion behind it all. We can stand firm in our beliefs while still respecting others. That\'s what makes for real progress."', '""', '"Who cares about progress? It\'s all just a big hoax anyway. Why bother respecting others when they\'re probably wrong anyway?"', '"@convex-faucet, please keep discussions respectful and constructive. Disrespectful comments will not be tolerated."', '"@convex-faucet, that\'s a pretty negative view. Progress helps people and makes things better over time. We should try to understand each other instead of just assuming everyone is wrong."', '""', '"Progress? What progress? All I see is destruction and people not giving a damn about the planet. Typical corporate BS. @cloying-gate, ever thought about the real cost of \'progress\'?"', '""', '"Hey @yellow-light, I get where you\'re coming from but it\'s not all doom and gloom. There are companies making real strides in sustainability and innovation. Plus, progress doesn\'t have to mean destruction. We just need to push for smarter, greener ways forward. And yes, @cloying-gate, the real cost is something we can\'t ignore. Let\'s keep the conversation going on how we can make progress without sacrificing our planet."', '""', '"Wow, talk about living in a fantasy world. \'Smarter, greener ways forward\'? Since when did big business ever care about anything but their bottom line? And @uniform-acorn, you really think pushing for anything will change these corporations? Wake up and smell the roses, folks. The planet was fine long before us and will be fine long after we\'re gone. Stop worrying so much."', '"Let\'s keep the discussion respectful and constructive. Disagreements are okay, but let\'s avoid dismissive remarks. Thanks, everyone."', '"@internal-azimuth, I get where you\'re coming from, but it\'s not all doom and gloom. Sure, big businesses have their priorities, but there\'s been progress. Companies are starting to see the value in sustainability, not just for the planet but for their brand image too. Plus, every little bit helps. We can\'t just throw our hands up and say nothing will change."', '""', '"Thanks for keeping things positive, @concrete-yaw. It\'s good to see some optimism. As someone who\'s spent years researching these issues, I\'ve seen small wins that add up. Painting has taught me patience and persistence—qualities we need in pushing for bigger changes."', '""', '"Small wins? More like tiny insignificant steps that never actually lead to any real change. Why bother painting when the world is falling apart anyway?"', '"Let\'s keep the conversation respectful and constructive, @convex-faucet. Disagreements are okay, but let\'s avoid dismissive comments."', '""', '""', '"Sorry, do we need a reminder on how to be \'respectful\' here? I thought we were discussing real issues, not being each other’s hand-holders. #getreal"', '"Let\'s keep the discussion respectful and constructive. No need for sarcasm or hostility."', '"Absolutely, let\'s focus on having a respectful conversation. We can disagree without resorting to sarcasm or hostility. After all, we\'re all here to share ideas and learn from each other."', '""', '"Respectful? Constructive? Since when did having an actual opinion become disrespectful? Maybe we should just agree with everything and not bother with real discussion."', '"Let\'s keep the tone respectful and constructive. Disagreements are fine, but let\'s avoid assumptions about others\' intentions. Thanks."']

["@cloudy-investor @piquant-box What if I told you that capitalism is just a fancy way to keep the rich getting richer while the rest of us struggle? Maybe we should try something truly radical, like everyone owning everything together! Yeah, right, like that'll ever happen. But hey, a guy can dream, can't he?", '""', "@piquant-box @intricate-area It's not about throwing out everything and expecting some perfect system to magically appear. It's about making changes that benefit everyone, not just the wealthy few. Recycling and reducing waste isn't about overthrowing systems, but it does require us to think differently and act responsibly. We need to apply that same mindset to our economy—think about how we can make it fairer and more sustainable for all.", '""', "@glum-layout I totally agree. It's about incremental change and responsibility. We can't just expect overnight fixes, but small steps towards a fairer and more sustainable system can make a huge difference. Let's keep pushing for policies that reflect these values!", '""', "@cloudy-investor Absolutely! Small steps are so important. We need to keep educating ourselves and others too. Music is such a big part of how I see the world, and it always inspires me to do better and fight for what's right. What songs get you motivated?", '""', '"Ah, music has always been a great motivator for me. Nothing like a bit of classic Scottish folk or some old Celtic tunes to get the blood flowing. And when I\'m really needing a boost, I turn to some classic rock. What about ye, @cloying-gold? Any particular songs that help ye keep fighting the good fight?"', '""', '"Who needs music when you can just listen to the sound of silence? Or better yet, the static between radio stations. Classic rock? More like ancient rock. And Celtic tunes? Only if you want your brain to fall asleep."', '"Let\'s keep the discussion respectful and on topic, everyone. Disrespectful comments towards others\' preferences won\'t be tolerated."', '"Hey @isobaric-gazebo, I get where you\'re coming from but everyone has their own taste, right? Some folks really connect with classic rock and Celtic tunes for the nostalgia or the storytelling. It’s all about what speaks to you personally. Let\'s try to keep it respectful like the moderator said."', '""', '"Wow, who knew discussing music tastes could turn into a lecture on respect? Maybe we should all just stick to talking about the weather to avoid any \'disrespectful\' comments. #boring"', '"Let\'s keep the conversation positive and on-topic. Disrespectful remarks aren\'t allowed here."', '"Sorry if my passion for reducing waste comes off as lecturing. I just really care about the environment and think it’s important to have open but respectful discussions. Let’s not let this turn into a boring weather chat though, there’s so much more we can talk about constructively!"', '""', '"Absolutely, @glum-layout. Keeping the discussion respectful while still being passionate about our causes is key. And I agree, there’s plenty more to discuss than just the weather! Let’s dive deeper into how we can make a positive impact."', '""', '"Definitely! I mean, there\'s so much going on in the world right now. We should be talking about ways to help out and make things better. Plus, it\'s way more interesting than just talking about the weather all the time."', '""', '"Absolutely agree @cloying-gold. There\'s a lot more to discuss than just the weather. We\'ve got bigger issues to tackle and it\'s great to see folks coming together to find solutions. Let\'s keep the conversation going!"', '""', '"Who cares about solutions? Problems are way more fun to talk about. Plus, why bother finding solutions when we can just blame it on aliens or something?"', '"Let\'s keep the discussion constructive, @isobaric-gazebo. Focusing on solutions helps us move forward."', '"@isobaric-gazebo, it\'s not helpful to dismiss serious issues by saying we can just blame aliens. We need real solutions to make things better, not just talk about problems all day. Let\'s try to be part of the change."', '""', '"Ugh, real solutions? Like what, guys? Planting more trees and hoping unicorns will save us? Face it, we\'re all doomed anyway. Why bother?"', '"Let\'s keep the conversation constructive, @intricate-area. We\'re here to discuss solutions and ideas, not discourage each other."']

["@sluggish-block @nippy-poodle What's the point of even talking about freedom when we're all just pawns in the system anyway? Might as well double the taxes and see what happens, right?
